item.description: President Donald Trump is concluding his high-stakes visit to China and is already declaring it a success. The summit’s results so far are still unclear with no announcements of any new trade deals or purchases of American products. Trump suggested that airplane maker Boeing struck a deal to sell more aircrafts to Chinese companies, but offered few details. NBC’s Garrett Haake reports and moderator of Meet the Press Kristin Welker joins TODAY with analysis.

media:content.media:title: Trump Wraps China Summit With Xi Jinping: What Are the Results?
media:content.media:description: President Donald Trump is concluding his high-stakes visit to China and is already declaring it a success. The summit’s results so far are still unclear with no announcements of any new trade deals or purchases of American products. Trump suggested that airplane maker Boeing struck a deal to sell more aircrafts to Chinese companies, but offered few details. NBC’s Garrett Haake reports and moderator of Meet the Press Kristin Welker joins TODAY with analysis.

item.title: Trump Wraps China Summit With Xi Jinping: What Are the Results?

item.description: Chinese President Xi Jinping gave President Trump a rare, private tour of Beijing’s walled-off Zhongnanhai compound during the two-day summit. A hot mic captured Trump asking Xi if other foreign leaders were also received in the compound.

media:content.media:title: Xi gives Trump rare tour of secret garden at heart of Chinese government
media:content.media:description: Chinese President Xi Jinping gave President Trump a rare, private tour of Beijing’s walled-off Zhongnanhai compound during the two-day summit. A hot mic captured Trump asking Xi if other foreign leaders were also received in the compound.

item.title: Xi gives Trump rare tour of secret garden at heart of Chinese government

item.description: In the latest round of panda diplomacy playing out between the U.S. and China, NBC News’ Janis Mackey Frayer got exclusive access to the two pandas getting ready for their upcoming journey to Zoo Atlanta in America.

media:content.media:title: Exclusive look at Chinese pandas preparing for trip to America
media:content.media:description: In the latest round of panda diplomacy playing out between the U.S. and China, NBC News’ Janis Mackey Frayer got exclusive access to the two pandas getting ready for their upcoming journey to Zoo Atlanta in America.

item.title: Exclusive look at Chinese pandas preparing for trip to America

item.dc:creator: Abigail Williams
item.description: After living without their loved ones for more than a decade, two families of Americans detained in China are appealing to President Donald Trump to bring them home as he meets with Chinese leader Xi Jinping in Beijing.

media:content.media:text: Cynthia and Nelson Wells, Sr. with a photo of Nelson, Sr. and their son Nelson Wells, Jr., who’s been imprisoned in China since 2014, in their home in Haughton, La.
media:content.media:description: Cynthia and Nelson Wells Sr. with a photo of their son, Nelson Wells Jr., who’s been imprisoned in China since 2014.

media:content.media:text: Nelson Wells Jr.
media:content.media:description: Cynthia and Nelson Wells Sr. with a photo of their son, Nelson Wells Jr., who’s been imprisoned in China since 2014.

media:content.media:text: Dawn Michelle Hunt with her mother and older brother in Chicago in 1991.
media:content.media:description: Cynthia and Nelson Wells Sr. with a photo of their son, Nelson Wells Jr., who’s been imprisoned in China since 2014.

item.title: Families of Americans imprisoned in China appeal to Trump for help

item.description: CIA Director John Ratcliffe traveled to Cuba to "personally deliver President Trump's message" to Cuban leaders that the United States is ready to engage in discussions on economic and security issues.

media:content.media:title: CIA director travels to Havana to meet with Cuban officials
media:content.media:description: CIA Director John Ratcliffe traveled to Cuba to "personally deliver President Trump's message" to Cuban leaders that the United States is ready to engage in discussions on economic and security issues.

item.title: CIA director travels to Havana to meet with Cuban officials

item.dc:creator: Mike Hixenbaugh
item.description: President Donald Trump’s Medicaid cuts and emerging attacks on paid family caregiving have people fearing financial ruin as they fight to keep loved ones out of institutions.

media:content.media:text: Melissa Gonce sits on a bench on a porch next to Jason, she has her hand on his chin as he holds an orange toy
media:content.media:description: Melissa Gonce cares full time for her 28-year-old son, Jason, through a Medicaid program facing major cuts. She expects to lose $18,000 a year.

media:content.media:text: Jason stands on a set of stairs inside of his home
media:content.media:description: Melissa Gonce cares full time for her 28-year-old son, Jason, through a Medicaid program facing major cuts. She expects to lose $18,000 a year.

media:content.media:text: A four image composite: top left image shows a pill being poured out of a pill bottle onto a hand, top right shows Melissa helping Jason put on a shirt, bottom left show Melissa preparing food on a countertop, and bottom right shows Melissa shaving Jason's face
media:content.media:description: Melissa Gonce cares full time for her 28-year-old son, Jason, through a Medicaid program facing major cuts. She expects to lose $18,000 a year.

media:content.media:text: Melissa Gonce wheels her son Jason, in a wheelchair, on a sidewalk outside in a residential area
media:content.media:description: Melissa Gonce cares full time for her 28-year-old son, Jason, through a Medicaid program facing major cuts. She expects to lose $18,000 a year.

media:content.media:text: Melissa Gonce's hands are seen holding one of Jason's hands, there is a bump on the knuckle of his middle finger
media:content.media:description: Melissa Gonce cares full time for her 28-year-old son, Jason, through a Medicaid program facing major cuts. She expects to lose $18,000 a year.

media:content.media:text: A large crowd of people hold signs against medicaid cuts outside
media:content.media:description: Melissa Gonce cares full time for her 28-year-old son, Jason, through a Medicaid program facing major cuts. She expects to lose $18,000 a year.

media:content.media:text: A hand holds up a cellphone displaying a photo of Jason Gonce seated in a baby chair as a baby
media:content.media:description: Melissa Gonce cares full time for her 28-year-old son, Jason, through a Medicaid program facing major cuts. She expects to lose $18,000 a year.

media:content.media:text: A three image composite: the image on the left shows two black t-shirt's for Casa Mia's restaurant hanging on a hanger on a door, middle image shows Melissa embracing Jason, right image shows bills and coupons on a countertop
media:content.media:description: Melissa Gonce cares full time for her 28-year-old son, Jason, through a Medicaid program facing major cuts. She expects to lose $18,000 a year.

media:content.media:text: Michele Gregory and her partner and son (in a wheelchair) pose for a family portrait outside
media:content.media:description: Melissa Gonce cares full time for her 28-year-old son, Jason, through a Medicaid program facing major cuts. She expects to lose $18,000 a year.

media:content.media:text: Casey Barrett and his son hug his daughter, center, who is seated in a wheelchair, she holds two teddy bears, they are all in a field
media:content.media:description: Melissa Gonce cares full time for her 28-year-old son, Jason, through a Medicaid program facing major cuts. She expects to lose $18,000 a year.

media:content.media:text: Anna Keyzer and family members pose by a tree on a sidewalk for a family portrait
media:content.media:description: Melissa Gonce cares full time for her 28-year-old son, Jason, through a Medicaid program facing major cuts. She expects to lose $18,000 a year.

media:content.media:text: Melissa Gonce shaves Jason's chin in their bathroom
media:content.media:description: Melissa Gonce cares full time for her 28-year-old son, Jason, through a Medicaid program facing major cuts. She expects to lose $18,000 a year.

media:content.media:text: Jason, left, playing with a blue toy, sits next to his brother,
media:content.media:description: Melissa Gonce cares full time for her 28-year-old son, Jason, through a Medicaid program facing major cuts. She expects to lose $18,000 a year.

media:content.media:text: Melissa Gonce seated at her kitchen island
media:content.media:description: Melissa Gonce cares full time for her 28-year-old son, Jason, through a Medicaid program facing major cuts. She expects to lose $18,000 a year.

media:content.media:text: Melissa Gonce and her two sons walk down the stairs, seen from their backs
media:content.media:description: Melissa Gonce cares full time for her 28-year-old son, Jason, through a Medicaid program facing major cuts. She expects to lose $18,000 a year.

item.title: Families caring for disabled relatives face unthinkable choices as Medicaid cuts loom

item.description: Republican mayoral candidate Spencer Pratt reposted an AI-generated video depicting him as a Batman-inspired character saving a burning Los Angeles from his opponents, raising questions about how AI is reshaping elections. NBC News’ Steve Patterson reports.

media:content.media:title: AI-generated video supporting Spencer Pratt for mayor of L.A. goes viral adding to concern over fake campaign ads
media:content.media:description: Republican mayoral candidate Spencer Pratt reposted an AI-generated video depicting him as a Batman-inspired character saving a burning Los Angeles from his opponents, raising questions about how AI is reshaping elections. NBC News’ Steve Patterson reports.

item.title: AI-generated video supporting Spencer Pratt for mayor of L.A. goes viral adding to concern over fake campaign ads

item.dc:creator: Andrew Greif
item.description: Kansei Matsuzawa, 27, became the first Japanese-born player ever signed by an NFL team when he agreed to a deal as an undrafted free agent with the Raiders.

media:content.media:text: An illustration depicting Kansei Matsuzawa kicking a football on an orange background surrounded by stars
media:content.media:description: Kansei Matsuzawa, nicknamed "the Tokyo Toe" by a University of Hawaii teammate, is a kicker for the Las Vegas Raiders.

media:content.media:text: Kansei Matsuzawa prepares to kick a football
media:content.media:description: Kansei Matsuzawa, nicknamed "the Tokyo Toe" by a University of Hawaii teammate, is a kicker for the Las Vegas Raiders.

media:content.media:text: Kansei Matsuzawa in his Hawaii Rainbow Warriors uniform on the football field
media:content.media:description: Kansei Matsuzawa, nicknamed "the Tokyo Toe" by a University of Hawaii teammate, is a kicker for the Las Vegas Raiders.

media:content.media:text: Kansei Matsuzawa walks on stage with a large Japanese flag draped over his shoulder
media:content.media:description: Kansei Matsuzawa, nicknamed "the Tokyo Toe" by a University of Hawaii teammate, is a kicker for the Las Vegas Raiders.

item.title: YouTube taught a Japanese teen how to kick field goals. Now he’s in the NFL.

item.description: As album rollouts continue to evolve, fans recently attempted to melt a giant ice block in the middle of Toronto as part of Drake's "Iceman" announcement. But how did we get here? NBC News' Gadi Schwartz takes a look back at other memorable rollouts, and how they have advanced through the years.

media:content.media:title: The evolution of album rollouts
media:content.media:description: As album rollouts continue to evolve, fans recently attempted to melt a giant ice block in the middle of Toronto as part of Drake's "Iceman" announcement. But how did we get here? NBC News' Gadi Schwartz takes a look back at other memorable rollouts, and how they have advanced through the years.

item.title: The evolution of album rollouts

item.dc:creator: Tom Llamas
Dawn Liu
Mithil Aggarwal
item.description: Beijing has put the robotics industry front and center of its national agenda as the tech race with Washington heats up in several key areas, including AI.

media:content.media:text: X-Humanoid in Beijing.
media:content.media:description: A bionic face prototype at X-Humanoid in Beijing.

media:content.media:text: A production line at X-Humanoid in Beijing.
media:content.media:description: A bionic face prototype at X-Humanoid in Beijing.

media:content.media:text: The X-Humanoid robotics lab, top, and humanoids on display, below.
media:content.media:description: A bionic face prototype at X-Humanoid in Beijing.

item.title: Inside China’s race to dominate humanoid robotics industry

item.dc:creator: Matt Lavietes
item.description: An Oregon emergency room physician was sentenced to prison on Tuesday after hitting a man with his car and leaving him for dead

media:content.media:text: Silverton Hospital in Silverton, Ore.
media:content.media:description: Silverton Hospital in Silverton, Ore.

item.title: Oregon emergency room doctor sentenced to prison after hit-and-run

item.dc:creator: Ryan J. Reilly
Gabe Gutierrez
item.description: The Justice Department is pushing to indict 94-year-old Raúl Castro, the former president of Cuba, according to two U.S. official familiar with the matter.

media:content.media:text: Raul Castro.
media:content.media:description: Raúl Castro in Havana on May 1.

item.title: DOJ pushing to indict Raúl Castro over 1996 downing of civilian planes, officials say

item.dc:creator: Adam Edelman
item.description: As Becerra’s gubernatorial campaign touts his decades of experience, he’s drawn criticism for his tenure at the Department of Health and Human Services.

media:content.media:text: Vice President Harris Meets With The Task Force On Reproductive Health Care Access
media:content.media:description: Former Health and Human Services Secretary Xavier Becerra is polling at the top of the crowded California governor's race.

item.title: Xavier Becerra faces pushback from Biden-era colleagues as he rises in California

item.dc:creator: Steve Kopack
item.description: Government bonds around the world sold off sharply Friday as the price of oil jumped, both driven by developments in the Iran war, politics and trade.

media:content.media:text: US Plans Record $100 Billion Bill Sale As Borrowing Needs Mount
media:content.media:description: The yield on a 30-year U.S. Treasury bond rose above 5.12%, its highest level in nearly a year.

media:content.media:text: U.S. President Trump Meets With China's President Xi At Zhongnanhai
media:content.media:description: The yield on a 30-year U.S. Treasury bond rose above 5.12%, its highest level in nearly a year.

item.title: Global bonds sink and oil prices jump, triggering a stock sell-off

item.dc:creator: Lawrence Hurley
item.description: Supreme Court Justice Samuel Alito is pushing back on calls to step aside in a major upcoming case involving climate change due to his oil and gas stock.

media:content.media:text: Supreme Court Justice Samuel Alito
media:content.media:description: Supreme Court Justice Samuel Alito in 2021.

item.title: Justice Alito pushes back on calls to sit out a major Supreme Court climate case

item.dc:creator: Marlene Lenthang
item.description: Mackenzie Shirilla was dubbed “Hell on Wheels” for a devastating 2022 Ohio crash in which she drove into a brick building at 100 mph, killing her boyfriend and his friend

media:content.media:text: Mackenzie Shirilla cries as she's found guilty of murder in the death of her boyfriend in Cleveland on Aug. 14, 2023.
media:content.media:description: Mackenzie Shirilla cries as she is found guilty of murder on Aug. 14, 2023.

media:content.media:text: Dominic Russo and Davion Flanagan
media:content.media:description: Mackenzie Shirilla cries as she is found guilty of murder on Aug. 14, 2023.

media:content.media:text: Mackenzie Shirilla was sentenced to life in prison with first eligibility for parole after 15 years for intentionally crashing her car and killing her two passengers in Strongsville, Ohio.
media:content.media:description: Mackenzie Shirilla cries as she is found guilty of murder on Aug. 14, 2023.

media:content.media:text: THE CRASH - Movie Set
media:content.media:description: Mackenzie Shirilla cries as she is found guilty of murder on Aug. 14, 2023.

media:content.media:text: Dominic Russo
media:content.media:description: Mackenzie Shirilla cries as she is found guilty of murder on Aug. 14, 2023.

item.title: Who is Mackenzie Shirilla? The teen who killed her boyfriend in 100 mph crash

item.dc:creator: Jason Abbruzzese
Christine Romans
item.description: Zillow CEO Jeremy Wacksman on AI coming to the real estate industry. Buyers and sellers today have far more information than they did a few decades ago.

media:content.media:text: Zillow website.

item.title: Zillow CEO Jeremy Wacksman is preparing for the next revolution in real estate

item.dc:creator: Rebecca Cohen
item.description: There are no known cases of hantavirus in the United States as of Friday morning, garnering a sense of hope stateside amid a global outbreak that has killed three people, sickened 10 and forced dozens into quarantine.

media:content.media:text: Hantavirus Outbreak
media:content.media:description: Medical personnel escort U.S. passengers from the cruise ship MV Hondius, affected by a hantavirus outbreak, to awaiting shuttles at Eppley Airfield, in Omaha, Neb., on May 11, 2026.

media:content.media:text: Hantavirus Outbreak
media:content.media:description: Medical personnel escort U.S. passengers from the cruise ship MV Hondius to awaiting shuttles at Eppley Airfield, in Omaha, Neb., on Monday.

item.title: No known cases of hantavirus in U.S. as officials monitor 41 with possible exposure

item.dc:creator: Andrew Greif
item.description: Kansei Matsuzawa, 27, became the first Japanese-born player ever signed by an NFL team when he agreed to a deal as an undrafted free agent with the Raiders.

media:content.media:text: An illustration depicting Kansei Matsuzawa kicking a football on an orange background surrounded by stars
media:content.media:description: Kansei Matsuzawa, nicknamed "the Tokyo Toe" by a University of Hawaii teammate, is a kicker for the Las Vegas Raiders.

media:content.media:description: Kansei Matsuzawa, nicknamed "the Tokyo Toe" by a University of Hawaii teammate, is a kicker for the Las Vegas Raiders.

item.title: YouTube taught a Japanese teen how to kick field goals. Now he’s in the NFL.

item.dc:creator: Mike Hixenbaugh
item.description: President Donald Trump’s Medicaid cuts and emerging attacks on paid family caregiving have people fearing financial ruin as they fight to keep loved ones out of institutions.

media:content.media:text: Melissa Gonce sits on a bench on a porch next to Jason, she has her hand on his chin as he holds an orange toy
media:content.media:description: Melissa Gonce cares full time for her 28-year-old son, Jason, through a Medicaid program facing major cuts. She expects to lose $18,000 a year.

media:content.media:text: Jason stands on a set of stairs inside of his home
media:content.media:description: Melissa Gonce cares full time for her 28-year-old son, Jason, through a Medicaid program facing major cuts. She expects to lose $18,000 a year.

item.dc:creator: Harry Rabinowitz
item.description: Controlling indoor humidity can prevent mold, mildew, allergies and overly dry skin, according to our experts. Shop their top picks from Frigidaire, Midea and more.

media:content.media:text: Pairing a dehumidifier with an air conditioner and air purifier is the best way to create ideal indoor air quality, according to our experts.
media:content.media:description: Pairing a dehumidifier with an air conditioner and air purifier is the best way to create ideal indoor air quality, according to our experts.

media:content.media:text: Pairing a dehumidifier with an air conditioner and air purifier is the best way to create ideal indoor air quality, according to our experts.
media:content.media:description: Pairing a dehumidifier with an air conditioner and air purifier is the best way to create ideal indoor air quality, according to our experts.

item.title: The best dehumidifiers of 2026, according to experts

item.dc:creator: Harry Rabinowitz
item.description: REI’s biggest sale of the year is a great time to find deals on big brands like REI Co-op, The North Face, Merrell, Black Diamond and more.

media:content.media:text: REI is one of the largest outdoor retailers nationwide, and this is its biggest sale of the year.
media:content.media:description: REI is one of the largest outdoor retailers nationwide, and this is its biggest sale of the year.

media:content.media:text: REI is one of the largest outdoor retailers nationwide, and this is its biggest sale of the year.

item.title: The 50+ best deals at REI’s Anniversary Sale
